articles.title: Gold (XAU/USD) rises slow and steady – In-depth Gold technical analysis
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) update: Gold (XAU/USD) remains remarkably resilient, consolidating within a wide $4,500 to $4,900 range despite a hot US CPI print and a surging Dollar. As the metals complex digests China’s 45-month high inflation and pivots toward the high-stakes Trump-Xi summit, explore our in-depth technical analysis for Gold’s next major move.

articles.title: Forex Today: Hot US CPI boosts US Dollar as Treasury yields climb
articles.expand: The US Dollar Index (DXY) rallied toward the 98.30 region on Tuesday as hotter-than-expected United States (US) inflation data boosted Treasury yields and reinforced expectations that the Federal Reserve (Fed) may keep interest rates elevated for longer.

articles.title: GBP/USD Price Forecast: Dark-cloud cover looms as bears eye 1.3500
articles.expand: GBP/USD retreats by over 0.55% on Tuesday amid political turmoil in the UK, as pressure mounts on Prime Minister Keir Starmer to step aside following cabinet resignations. Technically, the formation of a ‘dark-cloud cover’ suggests further downside if sellers drive the pair below 1.3500.

articles.title: UK Energy Min. Milband told cabinet ministers he is prepared to run for Labour leadership
articles.expand: Nearly 100 Labour ministers have called for the prime minister Starmer to resign. Wes Streeting is expected to meet with Starmer tomorrow morning and ask how he expects to solve the problems. If that leads to asking and getting Starmer to step down, Milband will run for the Labour leadership, but the process is a bit complicated. Below is the proce...

articles.title: ECB's Nagel: We still have quite a bit of inflation ahead of us
articles.expand: Joachim Nagel, President of the Bundesbank and member of the European Central Bank (ECB), told Handelsblatt on Tuesday that ECB rate hikes are becoming increasingly likely with the high inflation that is ahead of them.

articles.title: Fed’s Goolsbee: Don't think Fed independence is going away
articles.expand: Austan Goolsbee, President of the Federal Reserve (Fed) Bank of Chicago, said that without central bank independence, inflation will come roaring back at the Greater Rockford Chamber of Commerce in Rockford, Illinois on Tuesday.

articles.title: US Dollar Index jumps after hot US CPI data reinforces hawkish Fed outlook
articles.expand: The US Dollar Index (DXY), which tracks the Greenback’s value against a basket of six major currencies, climbs to a five-day high on Tuesday as stronger-than-expected US inflation data bolsters the hawkish Federal Reserve (Fed) outlook.

articles.title: Saudi Aramco warns the World of Fuel Crisis | India Infoline
articles.expand: The global oil crisis is shifting from a crude price shock to a full-scale fuel supply crisis as disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz threaten gasoline, diesel, and jet fuel inventories. Saudi Aramco warns that critically low fuel stocks could reignite inflation, disrupt transportation, and pressure import-dependent economies like India.

articles.title: UK consumer spending falls for first time since 2024 as Iran war bites

articles.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Extra gains still on the cards
articles.expand: The Aussie Dollar continues its march north almost unabated, lifting AUD/USD to the area of multi-year tops. For now, the pair’s positive outlook remains unchanged, propped up by elevated inflation in Oz and the RBA’s hawkish stance.
